publicV1.reason: Bind existing operations to exact typed registry metadata and runtime conformance checks.

mcp.reason: Derive existing tool contracts from the registry and add exhaustive adapter checks.

openapi.reason: Add conformance assertions against the exact registry; generated external behavior is unchanged.

apiPlayground.reason: Add projection conformance assertions; rendered external behavior is unchanged.

internalOnly.reason: Add repository hooks, scripts, OpenSpec linting, and CI enforcement.

protocolDifferences.detail: Idempotency-Key remains an HTTP-only retry contract and is not an MCP argument.
MCP preserves the historical text task handle alongside canonical structured content.
MCP metadata preserves its asynchronous polling guidance and explicitly identifies Idempotency-Key as an unmapped REST-only header.
REST retains its dedicated task-create throttle while MCP remains subject to the MCP transport limiter.
REST applies a dedicated per-principal catalog throttle while MCP remains subject to the MCP transport limiter; both retain shared catalog-service capacity controls.
Lifecycle SSE remains REST-only because MCP tools use request-response transport.

The MCP SDK high-level registrar requires a root object output schema, so tools/list advertises an object derived from every canonical SessionHistory union variant while runtime structured content remains canonically validated.
REST keeps a 204 empty body while MCP returns an explicit deletion acknowledgement.
Both adapters select from the same stable error semantics, while existing REST status and message bodies remain byte-compatible and do not gain a new code field.
Every REST operation explicitly validates its canonical success schema while preserving the established handler value; MCP structured content remains canonicalized.
MCP application-boundary failures preserve compatibility text and add a stable safe error envelope under the com.cloud-agent-platform/public-error result metadata key.
Malformed tool arguments rejected by the MCP SDK before adapter invocation retain the SDK text-only validation result; adapter-reached public failures carry stable metadata.
